title: Battle of Aden (2015)
strengths: Bounded city battle with at least one direct-vs-disease separation possibility.
limitations: Civilian death figure is partly tied to a pro-Hadi claim; UN figure mixes civilians and pro-Hadi fighters.
notes: Used cautiously, with disease deaths stripped from the pro-Hadi civilian total before entering the baseline path.

title: Bullets or Hunger? Civilian Harm and the United States’ Partnered War in Yemen
geographic_scope: Yemen, with city references including Aden and Marib
strengths: Serious human-rights and civilian-harm framing that helps separate direct urban harm from wider indirect crisis effects.
limitations: Not a clean city-by-city fatality ledger for the specific episodes retained here.
notes: Used mainly to discipline exclusion of indirect mortality and to justify city-focused episodeization.

title: Battle of Marib (2021-2022)
strengths: Provides a bounded Marib battle row rather than a national Yemen pseudo-episode.
limitations: Military fatality claims are highly partisan and not suitable as core estimates without triangulation.
notes: Useful for retaining Marib as a bounded episode while still keeping numerators conservative.
